title: Wire not as expected
body:
Hello all,

I'm using `Verilator 5.014 2023-08-06 rev v5.014` under `Linux scorbetta-linux 6.5.0-14-generic #14~22.04.1-Ubuntu SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon Nov 20 18:15:30 UTC 2 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux` through COCOTB version `1.8.1`.

I'm having issues with the following Verilog snippet:

```
assign ACK = (~WNR & sci_ack_fall) | (WNR & sci_ack_rise);
```

where `WNR` is declared as `input wire`, `ACK` is declared as `output wire`, `sci_ack_fall` and `sci_ack_rise` are declared as `wire` and are an output of a module instantiated in the top module.

The output of the simulation is shown in the figure. It is clear that the `ACK` wire shall be asserted, while it is not.

![Screenshot from 2024-02-15 16-43-02](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/assets/133970060/089ad7f0-39ad-4559-b4e1-ce9cab5c44e6)

Stranger also that promoting `ACK` to reg the simulation gives proper result (as can be seen with the `test_reg` signal).

```
reg test_reg;
always @(posedge CLK) begin
    test_reg <= (~WNR & sci_ack_fall) | (WNR & sci_ack_rise);
end
```

Any idea?

Thanks a lot
S
